title: The Way to God and How to Find It
description: The Way to God and How to Find It — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Way to God and How to Find It" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "The Way to God and How to Find It

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: "To the Reader"
memes.description: "In this small volume I have endeavored to point out the Way to God."

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "Copyright pages exist to tell you that you can’t do something."

memes.label: "Abraham"
memes.description: "The great patriarch of Israel whose faith made him a pilgrim and sojourner — Moody cites him as one who caught a glimpse of the heavenly city and lived accordingly."

memes.label: Absalom
memes.description: The rebellious son of King David, used by D. L. Moody as a cautionary example of misplaced forgiveness and the tragic consequences of backsliding.

memes.label: "Adam"
memes.description: "The first man, created by God and placed in Paradise — whose fall through unbelief introduced sin into the world. Moody uses Adam as the starting point for understanding redemption: humanity fell by unbelief and is restored by believing."

memes.label: All Entries
memes.description: A complete A–Z listing of all wiki entries in The Way to God and How to Find It, excluding chapters and list pages.

memes.label: "Angels"
memes.description: "Created heavenly messengers who serve God's purposes — in Moody's teaching, they are ministering spirits who announce Christ's birth and resurrection, execute divine missions, and, crucially, refuse worship, demonstrating that worship belongs to God alone."

memes.label: "Assurance of Salvation"
memes.description: "The confident knowledge that one is saved — a doctrine grounded in the promises of Scripture and the witness of the Holy Spirit, not in fluctuating feelings."

memes.label: Atonement
memes.description: A fundamental Christian doctrine concerning Christ's sacrificial death as the payment for sin; referenced by D.L. Moody as one of the essential truths of the Bible along with Repentance and Regeneration (006-001)(008-022).

memes.label: Babylon
memes.description: The ancient city and empire where the Israelites were taken into exile; referenced by D.L. Moody in connection with Daniel and as a symbol of the consequences of backsliding (004-018)(009-017).

memes.label: "Backsliding"
memes.description: "The spiritual declension of a believer from close fellowship with God — a condition Moody addresses in his final chapter, urging the backslider to return to the joy of salvation."

memes.label: "Blasphemy"
memes.description: "The sin of speaking against God or claiming divine prerogatives — in Moody's argument, the charge of blasphemy leveled against Christ confirms His divinity, for if He were merely human His claims would indeed be blasphemous."

memes.label: "Blessedness"
memes.description: "The state of divine favor and happiness that belongs to those who belong to God — in Moody's teaching, blessedness is inseparable from the new birth, the forgiveness of sins, and the restoration of the backslider, and is the experience of all who receive God's remedy for sin."

memes.label: "Calvary"
memes.description: "The site outside Jerusalem where Jesus Christ was crucified — the geographical focal point of Moody's entire message, where the love of God was supremely displayed and the work of salvation completed."

memes.label: 'I — "Love That Passeth Knowledge"'
memes.description: "If I could only make men understand the real meaning of the words of the apostle John—\"God is love,\" I would take that single text, and would go up and down th."

memes.label: "II — The Gateway Into the Kingdom"
memes.description: "There is no portion of the Word of God, perhaps, with which we are more familiar than this passage."

memes.label: "III — The Two Classes"
memes.description: "I now want to speak of two classes: First, those who do not feel their need of a Saviour who have not been convinced of sin by the Spirit; and Second, those who."

memes.label: "IV — Words of Counsel"
memes.description: "It is dangerous for those who are seeking salvation to lean upon the experience of other people."

memes.label: "V — A Divine Saviour"
memes.description: "We meet with a certain class of Enquirers who do not believe in the Divinity of Christ. There are many passages that will give light on this subject."

memes.label: "VI — Repentance and Restitution"
memes.description: "Repentance is one of the fundamental doctrines of the Bible. Yet I believe it is one of those truths that many people little understand at the present day."

memes.label: "VII — Assurance of Salvation"
memes.description: "“These things have I written unto you that believe on the name of the Son of God; that ye may know that ye have eternal life, and that ye may believe on the nam."

memes.label: "VIII — Christ All and in All"
memes.description: "Christ is all to us that we make Him to be. I want to emphasize that word “all."

memes.label: "IX — Backsliding"
memes.description: "There are two kinds of backsliders."

memes.label: Index of Chapters
memes.description: All nine chapters of The Way to God and How to Find It, with their key themes and associated entry pages.

memes.label: "Chicago"
memes.description: "The city where D. L. Moody built his church and conducted much of his ministry — the setting for several illustrative anecdotes in 'The Way to God.'"

memes.label: "Commandments"
memes.description: "The moral and spiritual directives of God — in Moody's teaching, keeping God's commandments is both the evidence of love for Christ and the ground of assurance, while the First Commandment's prohibition of idolatry provides the decisive argument for Christ's deity."

memes.label: Communion
memes.description: The spiritual fellowship and intimate relationship between the believer and God — in Moody's teaching, daily communion with the Lord is essential to the Christian life and the greatest safeguard against backsliding (008-019, 009-010).

memes.label: Index of Concepts
memes.description: A complete index of all concept entries in The Way to God and How to Find It, ordered alphabetically by label.

memes.label: "Conversion"
memes.description: "The instantaneous turning of a sinner to God through repentance and faith — a decisive change of direction that Christ declared essential for entrance into His kingdom."

memes.label: "Conviction of Sin"
memes.description: "The deep realization of one's guilt before God — an essential work of the Holy Spirit that precedes, but is distinct from, true repentance and conversion."

memes.label: "Creation"
memes.description: "The divine act by which God created the heavens and the earth — an event Moody references to argue that regeneration, being a new creation, must be God's work alone."

memes.label: Damascus
memes.description: The ancient Syrian city where Saul of Tarsus was converted on the road — Moody references Damascus in connection with Paul's sudden conversion and subsequent transformation (004-015, 007-061).

memes.label: Daniel
memes.description: Old Testament prophet who served God faithfully in Babylonian exile — Moody references Daniel as an example of God's power to keep His servants in hostile environments (004-018).

memes.label: "David"
memes.description: "The king of Israel whose misguided forgiveness of his rebellious son Absalom illustrates the necessity of repentance before reconciliation — a negative example Moody uses to show that God does not make the same mistake."

memes.label: "The Devil"
memes.description: "The chief adversary of God and man — in Moody's teaching, a real and personal enemy whose power is broken by Christ the Good Shepherd, whose accusations are silenced by justification, and whose temptations require constant vigilance and the armor of God to resist."

memes.label: "Dr. Brooks"
memes.description: "A minister from St. Louis whose definition of repentance Moody quotes approvingly — that repentance is the sinner's self-judgment in view of God's love displayed at the cross."

memes.label: D. L. Moody
memes.description: Dwight Lyman Moody (1837–1899), American evangelist, author of "The Way to God and How to Find It," and founder of the Moody Church and Moody Bible Institute.

memes.label: Egypt
memes.description: The ancient land of Israel's bondage and the place of Joseph's preservation — in D. L. Moody's teaching, Egypt symbolizes both God's providential care and the world from which believers are delivered (004-018, 008-007).

memes.label: "Elijah"
memes.description: "The great Old Testament prophet whose power to raise the dead Moody distinguishes from Christ's — Elijah called on God for the miracle, while Christ commanded life by His own authority."

memes.label: Elisha
memes.description: Old Testament prophet and successor to Elijah — Moody references Elisha in his argument for Christ's divinity, contrasting Elisha's derivative miracles with Christ's inherent power over death (005-023).

memes.label: "Eternal Life"
memes.description: "The gift of God to every believer in Jesus Christ — not merely endless duration of existence but a new quality of life that begins at the new birth and continues forever, secured by the promise of God."

memes.label: "Eve"
memes.description: "The first woman, created by God as Adam's companion — deceived by Satan into believing the lie that God does not love, and through whom sin entered human experience."

memes.label: Index of Events
memes.description: A complete index of all event entries in The Way to God and How to Find It.

memes.label: "Everlasting Life"
memes.description: "The unending life that God gives to all who believe on His Son — in Moody's teaching, everlasting life is a present possession received by faith, not merely a future hope, and is contrasted with the everlasting punishment that awaits those who reject the remedy God has provided."

memes.label: "Faith"
memes.description: "Faith is taking God at His word unconditionally — the hand that receives the blessing of salvation, involving knowledge, assent, and appropriation of Jesus Christ as personal Saviour."

memes.label: "Faith and Works in Moody's Theology"
memes.description: "An analysis of the dialectical relationship between faith and works in D. L. Moody's theology — salvation by faith alone, yet works as its necessary evidence."

memes.label: Featured Pages
memes.description: A showcase of the highest-quality pages in this wiki, curated for their depth of content, scriptural grounding, and cross-referencing density.

memes.label: "Forgiveness"
memes.description: "God's free and gracious pardon of sin, granted to those who truly repent — the expression of His mercy and love, and the gateway to reconciliation with Him."

memes.label: Gethsemane
memes.description: The garden at the foot of the Mount of Olives where Jesus prayed in agony before His arrest — Moody references Gethsemane to illustrate both Peter's downfall through spiritual slumber and Christ's tender love for His failing disciples (009-033, 009-036).

memes.label: "The Gospel"
memes.description: "The glad tidings of God's redeeming love in Jesus Christ — the message Moody preached and the power of God unto salvation, which demands the response of repentance and faith, and which brings light and rejoicing to those who receive it."

memes.label: "Grace"
memes.description: "The unmerited favor and kindness of God toward sinners — not earned by human effort but freely given through Jesus Christ."

memes.label: "Heaven"
memes.description: "The eternal dwelling place of God and the redeemed — a prepared place for a prepared people, entered only through the new birth."

memes.label: "Hell"
memes.description: "The eternal state of the lost who reject God's remedy for sin — in Moody's teaching, hell is a sobering reality glimpsed in the present misery of the unrepentant, a place prepared for the unprepared, and the destiny of those who refuse Christ's offer of salvation."

memes.label: "Holiness"
memes.description: "The progressive work of God in the believer's life — set apart from sin and conformed to the character of Christ through the lifelong process of sanctification."

memes.label: "Holy Spirit"
memes.description: "The third Person of the Trinity — sent by the Father and the Son to convince the world of sin, guide believers into truth, and seal the redeemed for eternal life."

memes.label: "Hope"
memes.description: "The confident expectation of future good grounded in the promises of God — not uncertain wishing but the assured outlook of those who have been born again and possess eternal life."

memes.label: Intercession
memes.description: The ongoing priestly work of Jesus Christ on behalf of believers, advocating for them at the right hand of God, as taught by D. L. Moody.

memes.label: "Isaiah"
memes.description: "The great Old Testament prophet whose writings Moody quotes to demonstrate God's unfailing love and the sufficiency of God's provision for salvation."

memes.label: James
memes.description: One of the twelve apostles, brother of John and son of Zebedee, who witnessed the Transfiguration and whose epistle is quoted by D.L. Moody on the subject of wisdom (004-044)(009-031)(009-036).

memes.label: Jeremiah
memes.description: Old Testament prophet known as the "weeping prophet," whose writings are frequently cited by D.L. Moody, particularly in addressing backsliders and the deceitfulness of the human heart (003-015)(009-003)(009-004)(009-006)(009-011).

memes.label: "Jerusalem"
memes.description: "The chief city of Judea and the religious centre of Israel — the setting for Christ's crucifixion, the birthplace of the church at Pentecost, and the city where Nicodemus served on the Sanhedrin."

memes.label: "Jesus Christ"
memes.description: "The divine Son of God and Saviour of the world — the central figure of D. L. Moody's preaching and the sole ground of salvation through His atoning death on the cross."

memes.label: "John the Baptist"
memes.description: "The forerunner of Jesus Christ whose one-text message — 'Repent ye; for the kingdom of heaven is at hand' — inaugurated the gospel call that Christ and the apostles would continue."

memes.label: Joseph (OT)
memes.description: Old Testament patriarch sold into slavery in Egypt who rose to become second in command to Pharaoh — Moody references Joseph as an example of God's sovereign preservation of His servants (004-018, 005-006).

memes.label: "Joy"
memes.description: "The deep gladness of soul that flows from salvation in Christ — a fruit of the Holy Spirit, restored through confession of sin, and independent of changing circumstances."

memes.label: "Judas Iscariot"
memes.description: "The disciple who betrayed Jesus Christ — a tragic figure whose remorse without true repentance led to suicide. Moody uses Judas to distinguish between remorse and genuine repentance, and to demonstrate the unchanging love of Christ."

memes.label: "Judgment"
memes.description: "God's righteous assessment of every soul — a reality from which the believer in Christ is delivered, having already passed from death unto life."

memes.label: "Justification"
memes.description: "The judicial act of God by which He declares a believing sinner righteous on the basis of Christ's atoning work — received by faith alone, not by works."

memes.label: "Kingdom of God"
memes.description: "The spiritual realm where God reigns — entered only through the new birth, prepared for a prepared people, and surpassing all earthly kingdoms in glory."

memes.label: "The Law"
memes.description: "God's revealed moral standard — in Moody's teaching, the law serves to reveal sin, stop every mouth, and drive the sinner to Christ, for no one can be justified by its works."

memes.label: Lazarus
memes.description: The brother of Mary and Martha whom Jesus raised from the dead after four days in the tomb — in Moody's argument for Christ's divinity, the raising of Lazarus demonstrates that Jesus possessed inherent power to impart life (005-026).

memes.label: "Love of God"
memes.description: "The central attribute of God's character — unchangeable, surpassing all human knowledge, and supremely demonstrated in the death of Christ at Calvary (001-004)."

memes.label: "Love of God"
memes.description: "The unchanging, unfailing, and unsearchable love of God for humanity, supremely demonstrated at the cross of Calvary — the central theme of D. L. Moody's opening chapter and the foundation of all Christian hope."

memes.label: "Mercy"
memes.description: "God's compassionate disposition toward sinners — His readiness to forgive, bless, and restore those who turn to Him in repentance."

memes.label: "Moses"
memes.description: "The great lawgiver of Israel through whom God gave the Law and, significantly for Moody, the one who lifted up the brazen serpent in the wilderness — the Old Testament type of Christ's crucifixion."

memes.label: "New Birth"
memes.description: "The supernatural work of God by which a sinner is made a new creation in Christ — not reformation but regeneration, not patching up the old nature but receiving a new one, without which no one can see the kingdom of God."

memes.label: "Nicodemus"
memes.description: "A ruler of the Jews and a Pharisee who came to Jesus by night — the recipient of Christ's definitive teaching on the new birth. Moody presents him as the archetype of the moral, religious person who nevertheless needs regeneration."

memes.label: Obedience
memes.description: The standard of perfect conformity to God's will, exemplified in Jesus Christ and contrasted with human disobedience in D. L. Moody's teaching.

memes.label: Index of Organizations
memes.description: A complete index of all organization entries in The Way to God and How to Find It.

memes.label: "Peace"
memes.description: "The inward rest and tranquility that comes from reconciliation with God through Jesus Christ — a fruit of the Spirit and a mark of genuine faith, distinct from the false peace of presumption."

memes.label: "Pentecost"
memes.description: "The day when the Holy Spirit descended upon the early church and Peter preached repentance with 'marvellous results' — the birth of the Christian church."
aliases: The Descent of the Holy Spirit

memes.label: "Perish"
memes.description: "The solemn alternative to eternal life in Moody's preaching — the state of final ruin from which Christ came to save sinners, set in sharp antithesis to the promise that 'whosoever believeth in Him should not perish, but have eternal life.'"

memes.label: Index of Persons
memes.description: A complete index of all person entries in The Way to God and How to Find It, ordered alphabetically by label.

memes.label: Pharaoh
memes.description: The king of Egypt during the time of Moses, known for his hardened heart and refusal to let the Israelites go, used by D.L. Moody as an example of false repentance (004-018)(006-004).

memes.label: Index of Places
memes.description: A complete index of all place entries in The Way to God and How to Find It.

memes.label: Praise
memes.description: The act of glorifying and thanking God — in Moody's teaching, praise is both the spontaneous response of the redeemed heart and the evidence of genuine conversion, transforming the tongue that once cursed into one that blesses (002-017, 002-027).

memes.label: "Prayer"
memes.description: "Communion with God and calling upon His name — a vital Christian practice that, while good in itself, Moody insists is not a substitute for repentance and the new birth."

memes.label: "Psalms"
memes.description: "The Old Testament book of sacred songs and prayers — frequently cited by D. L. Moody as a source of comfort, assurance, and instruction for believers on their pilgrim journey through this world."

memes.label: "Redemption"
memes.description: "The act of Christ by which He bought back sinners from the bondage of sin — not with gold or silver but with His own blood, restoring them to God as His possession."

memes.label: "Repentance"
memes.description: "A fundamental Christian doctrine — a sincere turning away from sin toward God, involving a change of mind, heart, and direction, accompanied by faith in Jesus Christ."

memes.label: "Restitution"
memes.description: "The practical outworking of genuine repentance — making right past wrongs by returning what was taken, confessing what was hidden, and repairing what was broken."

memes.label: "Resurrection"
memes.description: "The bodily rising of Jesus Christ from the dead — the event that vindicates His deity and guarantees the believer's future resurrection and present justification."

memes.label: "Righteousness"
memes.description: "The perfect moral standing before God that no one possesses by nature, but which is imputed to the believer through faith in Jesus Christ — exceeding even that of the scribes and Pharisees."

memes.label: "Salvation"
memes.description: "The free gift of eternal life received by faith in Jesus Christ — not earned by works but wholly accomplished by Christ on the cross and appropriated by believing."

memes.label: "Satan"
memes.description: "The personal enemy of God and man — in Moody's teaching, Satan is a cunning adversary with thousands of years of experience, whose chief aim is to keep sinners from Christ, to cast believers into doubt, and to bring about the downfall of the self-confident."

memes.label: Scripture References
memes.description: Pages with explicit scripture references in their frontmatter, showing the biblical foundation of entries in this wiki.

memes.label: "Sin"
memes.description: "The universal human condition of rebellion against God — a state from which no one is exempt and from which only faith in Jesus Christ can deliver."

memes.label: Tabernacle
memes.description: A biblical metaphor used by D. L. Moody in reference to the believer's earthly body as a temporary dwelling, and the eternal heavenly home promised through Christ.

memes.label: "The Apostle Paul"
memes.description: "The apostle to the Gentiles whose writings provide the core New Testament texts Moody cites for the doctrines of sin, faith, salvation, grace, and the love of God."

memes.label: "The Apostle Peter"
memes.description: "The chief apostle of Jesus Christ — a disciple whose denial of Christ was overcome by the unchanging love of his Lord, and who became the foremost preacher of repentance at Pentecost."

memes.label: "The Church"
memes.description: "The community of believers in Jesus Christ — which Moody distinguishes carefully from the kingdom of God: one may be baptized into the church without being born into the kingdom."

memes.label: "The Cross"
memes.description: "The cross of Calvary, where Jesus Christ died for sinners — the supreme demonstration of God's love, the ground of salvation, and the object toward which saving faith looks."

memes.label: "The Crucifixion"
memes.description: "The central event of Christian faith — the crucifixion of Jesus Christ at Calvary, where He laid down His life for sinners, and the supreme demonstration of the love of God."

memes.label: "The Pharisee and the Publican"
memes.description: "The two contrasting figures in Christ's parable — the self-righteous Pharisee who thanked God he was not like other men, and the humble publican whose simple prayer 'God be merciful to me a sinner!' brought justification."
aliases: The Two Men in the Temple

memes.label: "The Prodigal Son"
memes.description: "The central figure in Christ's parable of the forgiving father — Moody's supreme illustration that the love of the Father draws sinners home and that true repentance leads to joyful restoration."

memes.label: "The Word of God"
memes.description: "The inspired and authoritative revelation of God — the means by which sinners are convicted, the seed of the new birth, and the foundation of Christian faith."

memes.label: Most Referenced Pages
memes.description: Pages in The Way to God and How to Find It ranked by total citation count, showing the most interconnected concepts and figures.

memes.label: "Worship"
memes.description: "The act of ascribing worth and rendering homage to God alone — which, in Moody's apologetic argument, becomes a proof of Christ's deity, since Jesus Christ received worship without rebuke, an act that would be idolatrous if He were a mere man."

memes.label: Zaccheus
memes.description: A tax collector in Jericho who climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus and was converted upon meeting Him — in Moody's teaching, Zaccheus exemplifies sudden, genuine conversion evidenced by immediate restitution (004-008).
